subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Clear organization with headings, table of contents and conventional sequence (Aim, Data, Tests, Conclusion).
Coherent logical development following a recognisable narrative from introduction to conclusion.
Relevant content throughout with effective use of figures (e.g., measurement illustration) to support replication.
weaknesses: Sub-sections occasionally misplaced (e.g., recommendations inside analysis) and formatting glitches detract from seamless flow.
Verbose paragraphs and repetitive explanations (e.g., repeated definition of r) reduce conciseness.
Inconsistent table numbering, missing candidate number field and omitted page references limit polish.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration displays good organization and clear logical progression (band 3), with most content relevant. However, conciseness is affected by repetition (band 2). Overall, the work meets the âwell organised and coherentâ descriptor but falls short of exceptional seamlessness, warranting 3/4.

strengths: Appropriate use of terminology and notation (product-moment correlation, ANOVA, symbols Î¼, Ï, rÂ²) throughout.
Effective deployment of multiple representations: data tables, box-and-whisker plots, scatter plot with regression line, bell curve.
Generally clear explanations aligned with notation, enabling the reader to follow the mathematical arguments.
weaknesses: Minor errors in labelling (Q3 mislabelled as Q2; âslopâ for slope) and one contradictory statement about the null hypothesis.
Representations are not always integrated into the text and some calculations (e.g., outlier boundary, post-hoc Tukey results) are incomplete.
Occasional inconsistent values for r and missing inline references reduce precision.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The candidate uses appropriate mathematical language and notation with effective representations and clear communication (band 3 across strands). Minor lapses prevent full consistency or sophistication, justifying 3/4.

strengths: Personal sporting experience clearly motivates choice of variables and tests, showing independent thinking.
The narrative of club rejection and training goals provides genuine context that drives the exploration.
Selecting four different inferential techniques demonstrates initiative and creativity.
weaknesses: Analysis relies heavily on standard textbook procedures and GDC output, limiting originality.
The personal approach, while clear, does not permeate every section of the exploration.
No novel modelling or bespoke methodology beyond established coursework techniques.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows significant evidence of independent thinking, a clear personal approach and creativity (band 2 across strands), but does not achieve outstanding or pervasive innovation, warranting 2/3.

strengths: Reflects meaningfully on correlation coefficient and variance explained by the model.
Offers basic critique of sample size and suggests alternative significance levels.
Links statistical outcomes (variance explained, p-values) to practical football training insights.
weaknesses: Reflection is surface-level and does not probe statistical assumptions (e.g., linearity, homoscedasticity) in depth.
Recommendations lack focus on key limitations or biases in data collection.
Critical analysis remains general rather than deep evaluation of methodological robustness.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is meaningful with some critique and clear connections to understanding (band 2 across strands). However, it does not demonstrate the depth or critical rigour required for higher bands, so 2/3 is appropriate.

strengths: Employs a range of HL-level mathematics (descriptive stats, PMCC, ÏÂ² tests, ANOVA) effectively to address the research aim.
Mathematical procedures are mostly correct with only minor errors, and results are interpreted accurately (e.g., F-value, p-values).
Demonstrates good understanding throughout, justifying test choices and interpreting outcomes in context.
weaknesses: Minor inconsistencies (two values of r, mislabelled regression variables) and omitted details (degrees of freedom, step-by-step ÏÂ² formula).
Missing post-hoc Tukey table and justification for category cut-offs in ANOVA.
Occasional lack of full calculation steps reduces transparency.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration uses relevant, course-level mathematics effectively with minor errors and good overall understanding (band 4 across strands). It falls short of sophistication and full rigour for higher bands, so 4/6 is awarded.

strandSelections.reasoning: The idea, data collection and choice of multiple statistical tests come from the studentâs own sporting experience, demonstrating noticeable independent thinking, yet the analysis relies heavily on textbook procedures and GDC output, so the evidence is âsignificantâ rather than âoutstandingâ.

Throughout the exploration the student explains the purpose of each test, interprets p-values, and connects outcomes to the context, demonstrating good understanding across the work.  Occasional misconceptions (confusing acceptance vs failure to reject) prevent classification as âthorough with sophisticationâ, but the understanding is strong enough for the 4-mark band.

Numeric results are mostly correct and internally consistent; minor mismatches (r = 0.602 vs 0.638, small misquote of ÏÂ² critical criterion) constitute âminor errorsâ, so the standard of correctness aligns with the 4-mark band.

Most material is relevant to the stated aim, yet explanations are frequently repetitive (definitions of r, repeated criteria for rejecting Hâ) and some sentences are superfluous.  Because this adds noticeable length without advancing the mathematics, the work is only âmostlyâ concise.

Terminology such as âproduct-moment correlation coefficientâ, âANOVAâ, âTukey HSDâ, symbols (Î¼, Ï, rÂ²) and equation layout are largely correct.  Minor slips (mislabelling Q3 as Q2, âslopâ for slope) prevent the description âconsistent and accurate throughoutâ, but the language is mostly appropriate.

Explanations generally match the notation and figures, yielding clear communication; occasional inconsistencies (two different values for r, contradiction in conclusion sentence about Hâ) stop it short of full consistency, but overall clarity is good.

All mathematics (descriptive stats, PMCC test, ÏÂ² GOF and independence, one-way ANOVA with post-hoc) is squarely at HL AI course level and applied purposefully to address the aim; the selection is relevant and extends beyond a single technique, satisfying the âused effectivelyâ descriptor.

The student supplies several representations: tables of raw and derived data, box-and-whisker plots, scatter plot with regression line, bell curve and calculator screens.  These capture main features effectively, yet there is little integration (e.g., diagrams not referenced in text) and no residual plot or Tukey diagram, so the usage is âeffectiveâ rather than âsophisticatedâ.

The student reflects on the meaning of r, rÂ², and explains why other factors must influence kicking distance, displaying meaningful reflection.  However, the discussion stays fairly surface-level and does not probe statistical assumptions (e.g., linearity, equal variances) in depth.

Using four different inferential techniques (PMCC test, two chi-square tests, ANOVA with Tukey) for one data set shows initiative and some inventiveness, but the methods are standard coursework procedures and no novel modelling is attempted, giving âsignificantâ rather than âoutstandingâ creativity.

The report follows a recognisable narrative (introduction â procedures â analysis â conclusion) and the argument generally develops step-by-step from the aim to the testing of hypotheses.  Although some paragraphs are verbose and occasionally wander (e.g., digressions about âculture circumferenceâ), the reader can still follow the logical thread without major effort, so coherence is clear but not exemplary.

There is some critique (acknowledging sample size limits, recommending 1 % significance, suggesting control of environmental factors). These comments reveal analysis but not a deep evaluation of method robustness or potential bias, fitting âmeaningful analysis with some critiqueâ.

The student explicitly links statistical findings (36 % of variance explained, p-values) to practical understanding of football training, showing a clear connection to learning.  The enhancement of understanding is moderate, not âstrongâ, because links remain general.

Headings, a table of contents, numbered tables/figures and a conventional sequence (Aim, Data, Tests, Conclusion) show good organisation.  However, sub-sections are sometimes misplaced (recommendations appear inside the analysis section) and there are formatting glitches; consequently the structure is âwell organisedâ rather than âexceptionallyâ seamless.

Personal motivation is clear in the narrative about rejection from a club and the desire to improve shot power; this personal context drives the selection of variables and interpretation of results.  While genuine, it does not pervade every part of the exploration, placing it in the âclear personal approachâ level.

annotations.comment: The title is concise and clearly states the research focus on calf circumference and kicking distance, establishing immediate context.
The candidate number field is present but left blank; include your candidate number to comply with IB submission requirements.
The table of contents lists sections but omits page numbers; adding accurate page references will improve navigation.
The studentâs personal narrative effectively demonstrates motivation and context, enhancing engagement with the investigation.
The literature review cites general findings but lacks inline references; use precise citations to strengthen academic integrity.
The introduction is verbose and would benefit from paragraph breaks to improve readability and logical flow.
Figure 1 clearly illustrates the measurement technique for calf circumference, aiding replication of the procedure.
The aim statement could more explicitly define variables (e.g. âX = calf circumference, Y = kick distanceâ) for precise mathematical communication.
Procedures describe a valid dataâcollection protocol but mislabels the regression variables later; ensure consistent assignment of dependent and independent variables.
A rigorous combination of table summaries and descriptive text supports replicability, showing thorough use of raw data in analysis.
Table 1 includes summed columns without footnotes explaining their derivation; add a brief note on how Î£XÂ² and Î£YÂ² were computed.
Quartile notation is inconsistent (Q2 used instead of Q3); maintain standard labels (Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4) to avoid confusion.
The box-and-whisker diagrams effectively reveal data dispersion and potential outliers, aiding exploratory analysis.
The lower outlier boundary calculation stops at the expression; include the numeric result for Q1 â 1.5ÃIQR to complete the analysis.
Justifies choice of the Pearson correlation by referencing linearity in the scatter diagram, demonstrating sound mathematical reasoning.
Phrasing such as âwill be inventedâ is awkward; revise to âwill be appliedâ to maintain precise mathematical terminology.
The regression line is correctly displayed, but âslopâ should be âslopeâ to use accurate mathematical terminology.
Two different values of r (0.6016 vs. 0.6380) appear without explanation of discrepancy; reconcile or comment on rounding differences.
The Pearson formula is presented but missing a clear denominator grouping; format as $$r=\frac{...}{\sqrt{...}\times\sqrt{...}}$$ for clarity.
Change âaccept the null hypothesisâ to âfail to rejectâ when p > significance; this aligns with standard statistical language.
Degrees of freedom are not stated for the correlation test; include df = nâ2 to complete the hypothesis test details.
Chi-square GOF test description is clear but omits the calculated ÏÂ² statistic and df; show these to substantiate your conclusion.
Tables 2â5 switch numbering inconsistently; maintain sequential table numbers and captions for better organization.
Correctly interprets ÏÂ² = 0.498 and p = 0.779 to accept the null; this shows accurate execution of GOF testing.
Expected frequencies in the two-way ÏÂ² test are recalculated but the ÏÂ² formula and sum are missing; include calculation steps.
Category cutoffs for small, medium, and large groups in ANOVA are not justified; explain choice based on data distribution or theory.
The ANOVA results (F = 16.21, p = 0.0000237) are correctly interpreted, showing a significant difference across calfâsize groups.
Tukeyâs HSD is mentioned but no post-hoc table is provided; include the Tukey comparison table with group means and confidence intervals.
Recommendations list many factors but lack focus on key statistical limitations; deepen reflection on potential biases and data constraints.
The conclusion effectively summarizes findings but contradicts earlier phrasing by stating âno relationshipâ then âdependentâ; clarify the relationship direction.
References are inconsistently formatted and lack a standard citation style (e.g., APA); apply uniform formatting and include access dates where needed.
